sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Director at Sempra (NYSE: SRE) receives new phantom share award
feed.summary: Cynthia J. Warner, a director at Sempra (NYSE: SRE), was granted 297.71 phantom shares as part of her director compensation on April 1, 2026. These phantom shares, valued at $97.41 each, convert to Sempra common stock on a 1-for-1 basis and are immediately exercisable upon vesting. Following this award, Warner holds a total of 13,528.30 phantom shares, which includes 1,906.10 unvested restricted phantom shares that are subject to forfeiture under certain conditions.

feed.title: Director at Sempra (NYSE: SRE) receives 128 phantom shares award
feed.summary: Sempra director Michael N. Mears was awarded 128.32 phantom shares of Sempra common stock as director compensation on April 1, 2026, at a grant price of $97.41 per share. These phantom shares are immediately exercisable and convertible to common stock on a 1-for-1 basis, bringing his total holdings to 20,788.42 phantom shares, including 1,906.10 unvested restricted shares. The transaction, reported via a Form 4 filing, is considered a compensation-related acquisition rather than a market purchase or sale.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Invests US$1bn into South African Expansion
feed.summary: Coca-Cola has announced a R17.6 billion (US$1 billion) investment to significantly expand its production capacity and strengthen distribution networks in South Africa. This commitment highlights the company's belief in the country's economic potential and follows its decision to divest CCBA to Coca-Cola HBC as part of a global refranchising strategy. The investment aims to boost local value creation, support employment, and foster sustainable growth within the South African economy.

feed.title: The Precedent: Federal Circuit Upholds Gene Therapy Host Cell Claims Under 35 U.S.C. § 101 in Regenxbio Inc., Et Al. v. Sarepta Therapeutics, Inc., Et Al.
feed.summary: The Federal Circuit reversed a district court's summary judgment, ruling that gene therapy host cell claims in Regenxbio Inc., et al. v. Sarepta Therapeutics, Inc., et al. are patent-eligible under 35 U.S.C. § 101. The court determined that the claimed host cells, containing recombinant nucleic acid molecules, are "markedly different" from anything found in nature, aligning with the Chakrabarty decision. This ruling emphasizes that human-engineered biological inventions incorporating naturally occurring sequences can be patent-eligible if the composition as a whole is nonnaturally occurring and possesses distinct characteristics and utility.

feed.title: Award honors span post-quantum chips and cyber training at Lattice
feed.summary: Lattice Semiconductor (NASDAQ: LSCC) has been recognized with multiple 2026 Cybersecurity Excellence Awards. The company's MachXO5™-NX TDQ FPGA family won for its pioneering post-quantum cryptography capabilities, and the Lattice Cyber Resilience Program was honored for its comprehensive educational framework in cybersecurity awareness. These awards underscore Lattice's leadership in providing advanced cybersecurity solutions and its commitment to strengthening cyber resilience across various industries.

feed.title: Should New Data Center Optimism and Analyst Upgrades Require Action From NiSource (NI) Investors?
feed.summary: KeyBanc recently initiated coverage on NiSource (NI) with an Overweight rating, joining other firms with positive views, emphasizing the utility's exposure to growing data center power needs and supportive regulation. While these factors reinforce NiSource's growth story, the company continues to manage a significant debt load and tight interest coverage. Investors should consider both the potential from data center demand and the ongoing risks associated with its leverage and funding requirements.
